factors: If the farmer was moving towards the fruit and the wizard didn't add the rock, then the wizard didn't affect whether or not they would get the fruit. If the wizard blocked the passage, then he was either blocking them from one fruit or forcing them to choose another one. If he removed the rock, he was enabling them. If he didn't place the rock, he was allowing them or possibly enabling them if he had a choice to put the rock. If the farmer was moving in one direction and then stopped as the farmer placed the rock, I felt like the wizard kept the farmer from going to the fruit he really wanted because the farmer may have changed directions but didn't have a choice.
feedback: I think everything worked great. Everything was easy to understand and comprehend.
